summary: Provision a new AWS Network Firewall. Greenfield today (none exists yet in this estate) — the create stands up the firewall with one starter subnet mapping; the firewall policy (its rule groups) and routing traffic through it are engineer follow-ups.

operations.title: Provision a new network firewall
operations.summary: Request a brand-new AWS Network Firewall; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform, including its policy, before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new aws_networkfirewall_firewall as a complete specification: VPC, firewall policy and a starter subnet mapping. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: vpc
params.label: VPC
params.help: The VPC this firewall inspects traffic for.

params.name: firewall_policy_arn
params.label: Firewall policy
params.help: The engineer picks or authors the stateless/stateful rule-group policy during review — a firewall filters nothing without one.

params.name: subnet
params.label: Subnet (firewall endpoint)
params.help: One subnet the firewall places an inspection endpoint in. Add more Availability Zones afterward — this form seeds one starter mapping.

params.name: delete_protection
params.label: Delete protection
params.help: Always on for a new firewall — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Firewall name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this firewall is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this firewall.

decisions: The firewall policy (stateless default actions plus stateful rule groups) is an engineer decision — pick or author it before this firewall can meaningfully filter traffic; an empty/permissive policy passes everything
This form seeds exactly one subnet mapping (one Availability Zone); additional zones are an engineer follow-up
Routing traffic THROUGH this firewall (route table changes pointing at its VPC endpoints, in both directions) is a separate, required follow-up — creating the firewall alone redirects no traffic
